skill.name: pubmed-database
skill.description: Build and refine PubMed searches using Boolean logic, MeSH terms, field tags, and NCBI E-utilities API workflows.
skill.summary: Create precise PubMed queries and API workflows for biomedical literature research.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, credential, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance patterns, but manual review found markdown documentation and PubMed API examples rather than executable malicious code. The only confirmed risk factor is legitimate network use for NCBI E-utilities requests, so the skill is safe to publish with normal user caution about API keys and external HTTP calls.

low_findings.title: Static Command Findings Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The external command detections are false positives caused by markdown code fences and inline PubMed query syntax. The reviewed files contain documentation and examples, not executable shell or Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are markdown code blocks showing PubMed queries or API examples. No executable script file, shell invocation, or dynamic command execution was found.

low_findings.title: API Key References Are Placeholders
low_findings.description: The env_access detections reference the literal placeholder YOUR_API_KEY and E-utilities API parameters. The skill does not read environment variables or embed real credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines use placeholder API key text in documentation. No evidence found of process environment access, secret harvesting, or hardcoded live credentials.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto And Reconnaissance Matches Are Context False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance detections arise from biomedical terms, DOI examples, PubMed syntax, HTTP status documentation, and support contact information. No hashing, scanning, or reconnaissance code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious terms are ordinary PubMed documentation content. I did not find cryptographic function calls, scanners, or code that probes local or network systems.

low_findings.title: Legitimate NCBI Network API Usage
low_findings.description: The skill documents HTTP requests to the NCBI E-utilities service. This is expected for PubMed automation, but users should review generated code before sending queries or API keys to external services.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network destination is the documented NCBI E-utilities endpoint and support documentation. The behavior matches the skill purpose and is not hidden or unrelated.

content.value_statement: Biomedical literature searches can be slow and inconsistent when query syntax is unclear. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code build precise PubMed queries and NCBI E-utilities workflows.
actual_capabilities: Constructs PubMed queries with Boolean operators, field tags, MeSH terms, and publication filters.
Explains NCBI E-utilities endpoints such as ESearch, EFetch, ESummary, EPost, and ELink.
Provides query patterns for clinical trials, systematic reviews, epidemiology, genetics, and public health topics.
Guides citation lookup by PMID, DOI, author, journal, date, volume, and page metadata.
Outlines batch retrieval, history server workflows, rate limits, and citation export formats.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not a hosted PubMed search service.
Generated searches may need validation by a librarian, clinician, or subject expert.
Full text access depends on publishers, subscriptions, and open access availability.
API workflows require users to manage rate limits, user agents, and optional NCBI API keys.

use_cases.title: Plan a Systematic Review Search
use_cases.description: Convert a PICO question into a documented PubMed strategy with MeSH terms, synonyms, filters, and reproducible search steps.

use_cases.title: Automate Literature Retrieval
use_cases.description: Design an E-utilities workflow that searches PubMed, collects PMIDs, retrieves abstracts, and respects NCBI rate limits.

use_cases.title: Find Targeted Biomedical Evidence
use_cases.description: Build focused queries for trials, reviews, guidelines, author searches, DOI lookup, or recent topic monitoring.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic PubMed Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a PubMed query for recent research on [topic]. Include synonyms, field tags, date filters, and a short explanation.

prompt_templates.title: Refine a Clinical Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Improve this PubMed query for a clinical question: [query]. Add MeSH terms, publication type filters, and explain tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Systematic Review Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a reproducible PubMed search strategy for this PICO question: [question]. Include concepts, synonyms, MeSH terms, filters, and documentation notes.

prompt_templates.title: Plan an E-utilities Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an NCBI E-utilities workflow for retrieving PubMed records for [topic]. Include endpoint choices, batching, rate limits, and output formats.

output_examples.input: Find recent systematic reviews about type 2 diabetes treatments.
output_examples.output: A PubMed-ready search strategy using diabetes synonyms, MeSH terms, systematic review filters, date limits, and notes on result refinement.

output_examples.input: Retrieve abstracts for articles matching a cancer immunotherapy query.
output_examples.output: A workflow that searches PubMed, stores result history, retrieves abstracts in batches, and documents rate-limit precautions.

output_examples.input: I have a DOI and partial citation details for a journal article.
output_examples.output: A set of PubMed lookup options using DOI, author, journal title, publication year, volume, and page fields.

best_practices: Document the exact search string, database, filters, and search date for reproducibility.
Combine MeSH terms with title and abstract synonyms to improve recall and precision.
Use NCBI rate limits, batching, caching, and a descriptive user agent for API workflows.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on a single keyword when a topic has synonyms or controlled vocabulary terms.
Do not use narrow filters before checking whether relevant articles are being excluded.
Do not paste private API keys into shared prompts, logs, repositories, or published examples.

faq.question: Does this skill search PubMed directly?
faq.answer: It helps create searches and API workflows. Actual requests depend on the user environment and chosen tool.

faq.question: Can it help with MeSH terms?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains MeSH search patterns, major topic tags, subheadings, and how to combine them with keywords.

faq.question: Is it useful for systematic reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports PICO-based search planning, reproducible query documentation, and common evidence filters.

faq.question: Does it provide full text articles?
faq.answer: No. PubMed usually provides citations and abstracts. Full text depends on open access, publishers, or institutional access.

faq.question: Do I need an NCBI API key?
faq.answer: An API key is optional for many examples, but it increases permitted request rates for E-utilities workflows.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use this for Python workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes REST API guidance and notes that Bio.Entrez may be preferable for some Python projects.
